folio.label: 160r
folio.l: ¢ Nomore of this , for goddes dignytee

note.notetxt: 1 No indication that this could be another textual part. The links is written continuosly and with the same ink as TT.ODR

folio.l: Quod oure hoost , for thow makest me
So wery , of thyn verray lewednesse
That also wysly , god my soule blisse
Myne Eres aken , of thy drasty speche
Now swicú a ryme , the deuele I be teche
This may wel be ryme dogereÂ , quod he
¢ Why so q¶ I , why woltow lette me
More of my tale , than another man
Syn that it is , the best ryme I can
¢ By god quod he , for pleynly at a word
Thy drasty rymyngö , is nat wortú a tord
Thow doost nat elles , but despendest tyme
Sire at o word , thow shalt no lengere ryme
Lat se , wher thow kanst tellen ougút in geste
Or tellen in prose , somwhat atte leste
In whicú there be som myrthe , or som docténe

note.notetxt: 17the last four lines are crammed in.ODR
